behavior_summary: This file implements a collection of scroll-handler processes. Each named function prepares a fixed, hard-coded text string (a scroll's contents) and hands it to the game's scroll-display routine so the player can read the scroll.

Each handler pushes the reader context (the invoking actor/process id) and an object pointer, converts a literal into a runtime string pointer, calls the central display routine, frees the temporary string, then suspends/returns.
Handlers are purely data-driven: they present lore, clues, or mechanical hints (potion effects, reagents, letters, dying notes) and do not modify global game state or flags in these snippets.
notable_control_flow: Entry → prepare context (push reader pid, push [BP+06h] object_ptr) → convert literal to runtime string pointer → call external process Scroll::read(char*) → free temporary string → suspend/return.

All functions follow the same linear sequence and appear to yield/suspend after invoking Scroll::read, implying the UI presentation may be asynchronous or expects to yield control while the player reads.
There are no conditional branches or global-flag based guards observed in these handlers; they unconditionally present their stored text when invoked.
open_questions: What exact in-game event or action maps to invoking these handlers (e.g., 'use item', 'read item' or an 'enter area' trigger)? The disassembly implies 'read/activate scroll' but the exact engine event ID is not present.

Is process Scroll::read(char*) synchronous (returns after the player finishes reading) or asynchronous (returns immediately and UI driving is separate)? The suspend after call suggests asynchronous behavior but this is inferred.
Are any of the duplicate texts (for example the Sysaphus letter duplicates) intentionally duplicated for different scroll items, or is this a disassembly artifact?
Are there other side-effectful operations performed by related scroll scripts elsewhere (e.g., granting quest state) that are not visible in this file?

main_function.name: U8P_SCROLL1::0080
main_function.purpose: Present a fixed 'Shopping list' scroll text to the UI when the scroll is read/activated by a player/actor.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the corresponding scroll item is read/activated by a player or actor (inferred from the calling pattern and the call to process Scroll::read).
